changelog:
Fork patches A5 — drop the Claude-Code-detection replacement entirely:
- When FilterClaudeCode is enabled and a Claude Code CLI system prompt is detected, applySystemPromptFilters now returns an empty string instead of replacing with the static 'Help the user with their software engineering task...' line. The model fingerprinted that lone replacement line as fake injection because no real Claude Code request would send only that.
- Tool definitions and conversation context still travel via the structured fields (UserInputMessageContext.Tools, History, etc.); Claude on Bedrock falls back to its training defaults for response styling when no system prompt is provided.
- claudeCodeBackendPrompt constant kept as a no-op empty string for backward import compatibility; the previous replacement text preserved in claudeCodeBackendPromptLegacy for git-history reference.
- Settings UI hint (zh + en) updated to reflect the new 'drop entirely' behaviour.

A4: removed ThinkingModePrompt entirely; split applyPromptFilters into system + user variants; minimalFallbackUserContent changed from '.' to ''.
A3: removed --- SYSTEM PROMPT --- markers; dropped 'Tool results:' prose envelope; shortened claudeCodeBackendPrompt; tightened detector; realtime quotaTotal/quotaUsed.
A2: container_name kiro-go-patch + pull_policy: always.
A1: <system-reminder> block + x-anthropic-billing-header strips, /v1/responses endpoint, model echo, dashboard refresh.
